title: Layers panel component for design editor with tree view and selection
purpose: Provides a collapsible tree view of scene graph nodes for selecting and managing layers in the design editor. Supports visibility toggling and expand/collapse functionality.
module_context: Exports a React functional component LayersPanel that renders a tree view of scene graph nodes with type icons, visibility indicators, and selection handling. Uses Zustand for state management and zustand/react/shallow for shallow equality checks.

steps.content: Extract selected IDs, scene version, and current page ID from the design editor store.
Initialize a set to track expanded nodes.
Derive the layer items for display by walking the scene graph and building a flat list with depth information.
Define a callback to toggle node expansion state.
Define a callback to handle layer selection on click.
Define a callback to toggle node visibility.
Render the panel header with close button if provided.
Render the list of layers with appropriate styling and interactive elements.
Retrieve the parent node from the scene graph.
Skip processing if the parent node does not exist.
Reverse the order of child IDs to display top layers first.
Process each child node in the reversed list.
Skip processing if the child node does not exist.
Determine if the child node has children.
Add the child node information to the result list.
Recursively walk the child node if it has children and is expanded.
Create a new set of expanded IDs based on the previous state.
IF the ID is already in the set, remove it to collapse.
ELSE add the ID to expand it.
Update the expanded state with the new set.
Select the clicked layer ID in the design editor store.
Pass the shift key state to enable additive selection.
Stop event propagation to prevent parent handlers from triggering.
Retrieve the node from the scene graph.
Skip processing if the node does not exist.
Update the node's visibility in the design editor store with undo support.
